name: Budapest
localTips: Validate paper metro and tram tickets in the small red or yellow machines before boarding — plainclothes inspectors in dark vests check constantly and fines are collected on the spot.
Restaurants on Váci utca and around the castle charge tourist prices — walk a few streets into the Jewish Quarter or Buda's back streets for the same food at half the cost.
Thermal baths are a year-round local habit, not just a summer activity — many Hungarians go weekly, and weekday mornings are far less crowded than weekends.
Tap water in Budapest is safe and good to drink, so skip bottled water at restaurants and refill from public fountains around the city.
Ruin bars change character completely by time of day — visit Szimpla Kert's daytime farmers' market on Sundays for a totally different, family-friendly experience.

activities.title: Fisherman's Bastion terraces
activities.description: Neo-Gothic turrets on Castle Hill with the best free panorama over the Danube and Pest's skyline — the lower terraces are free; only the top-tier viewing platform charges.

activities.title: Széchenyi Chain Bridge walk
activities.description: Cross the 1849 suspension bridge guarded by stone lions, linking Buda and Pest — the classic postcard shot of Budapest, lit gold after sunset.

activities.title: Shoes on the Danube memorial
activities.description: Sixty pairs of iron shoes cast into the riverbank, a haunting memorial to Jewish victims shot into the Danube during WWII — a short, sobering stop along the promenade.

activities.title: Heroes' Square
activities.description: A monumental square marking Hungary's millennium, with statues of tribal chiefs and kings arced around a towering column — the grand gateway to City Park.

activities.title: Városliget (City Park)
activities.description: A vast green park behind Heroes' Square with the fairy-tale Vajdahunyad Castle, a boating lake that becomes an ice rink in winter, and shady avenues year-round.

activities.title: Great Market Hall browsing
activities.description: A vast 1897 iron-and-glass market hall near the Liberty Bridge — paprika stalls, embroidery and a lángos food court upstairs, free to wander floor to floor.

activities.title: Great Synagogue exterior & Jewish Quarter
activities.description: Europe's largest synagogue, its Moorish-revival facade towering over the old Jewish Quarter — now surrounded by ruin bars, murals and the district's layered history.

activities.title: Margaret Island
activities.description: A car-free island park in the middle of the Danube, with a musical fountain, ruins of a medieval convent and a running track locals use every evening.

activities.title: Andrássy Avenue walk
activities.description: A UNESCO-listed boulevard of grand 19th-century townhouses running from downtown to Heroes' Square, passing the Opera House and elegant embassy buildings.

activities.title: Citadella hilltop viewpoint
activities.description: A 19th-century fortress atop Gellért Hill with Budapest's single best panorama — both banks of the Danube, all its bridges, in one sweep.

activities.title: Parliament building from the Danube bank
activities.description: The neo-Gothic Parliament is best appreciated from Buda's riverside promenade or a passing tram — one of the largest and most photographed legislative buildings on earth.

activities.title: Christmas market at Vörösmarty tér
activities.description: Central Europe's most-awarded Christmas market — mulled wine, chimney cake stands and craft stalls fill the square through Advent.

activities.title: Gellért Hill slopes & lookout paths
activities.description: Wooded trails wind up Gellért Hill past the Cave Church entrance and old bunkers, giving hikers quieter photo spots than the crowded Citadella terrace above.

activities.title: Kőrút ring road tram ride (tram 4/6)
activities.description: One of the world's busiest tram lines circles the Nagykörút past Art Nouveau facades and buzzing squares — a cheap, unofficial city tour from a regular seat.

activities.title: Buda Castle District cobbled streets
activities.description: Wander the medieval streets around Matthias Church and the castle walls — pastel baroque houses, tiny courtyards and views down to the river at every turn.

activities.title: Coffee at the New York Café
activities.description: A gilded, chandeliered 1894 coffee house once dubbed the most beautiful café in the world — order a single coffee just to sit inside its marble-columned hall.
activities.insiderNote: Go right at 9am opening on a weekday to see the room without a wait — by mid-morning the queue snakes down the block.

activities.title: Szimpla Kert ruin bar
activities.description: The original ruin bar, built into a derelict building's rooms and courtyard with mismatched furniture, bathtub seating and a beat-up Trabant car for a bar.
activities.insiderNote: Go on a Sunday morning instead of at night to catch the farmers' and antique market it hosts in the same courtyard — a completely different scene.

activities.title: Gozsdu Udvar passage bar crawl
activities.description: A chain of seven interconnected courtyards linking two streets, lined with bars and restaurants — a covered, weatherproof route through the Jewish Quarter's nightlife.
activities.insiderNote: It's fully covered end to end, so it's the local go-to when the weather turns — duck in from either Király utca or Dob utca.

activities.title: Cave Church in Gellért Hill
activities.description: A working chapel built into a natural cave in the hillside, used as a secret hideout by monks during the communist era before being sealed and later reopened.
activities.insiderNote: Entry is a small donation, not a ticketed attraction — most tourists walk past the unmarked cave entrance on their way up to the Citadella.

activities.title: Ecseri flea market
activities.description: Central Europe's biggest flea market, out on the edge of the city — communist memorabilia, antique furniture and genuine junk that draws serious collectors.
activities.insiderNote: Take bus 54 from Boráros tér; Saturday mornings are when the best dealers set up, and haggling is expected.

activities.title: Lángos at a proper local stand
activities.description: Fried dough piled with sour cream, cheese and garlic is Hungary's ultimate street snack — skip the tourist stalls and find one where locals actually queue, like Retró Lángos.
activities.insiderNote: Order it 'sajtos-tejfölös' (with cheese and sour cream) — the classic combination, no need to over-order toppings.

activities.title: Rooftop hot tub at Rudas Baths
activities.description: A 16th-century Ottoman bathhouse with a modern rooftop pool added on top — soak with a night-time skyline view most visitors don't know exists above the historic domes.
activities.insiderNote: The rooftop pool is only accessible on certain mixed-gender nights and weekends — check the schedule, as many sessions are single-sex only.

activities.title: Fény utca market hall
activities.description: A Buda-side market hall used almost entirely by neighbourhood locals rather than tourists — fresh produce, cheap canteen lunches and none of the queues of the Great Market Hall.
activities.insiderNote: The upstairs canteen stalls serve a full Hungarian lunch for a few euros — point at what looks good, it's how regulars order.

activities.title: Hungarian Parliament interior tour
activities.description: Walk beneath the frescoed dome and see the Hungarian crown jewels inside one of the world's most ornate legislative buildings — book well ahead, tours sell out fast.
activities.bookingQuery: Hungarian Parliament interior guided tour Budapest

activities.title: Széchenyi Thermal Baths
activities.description: One of Europe's largest bathing complexes, with steaming outdoor pools set against a bright yellow neo-baroque palace — a Budapest institution used year-round, even in snow.

activities.title: Buda Castle Labyrinth tour
activities.description: A network of natural caves and cellars beneath Castle Hill, used as a wine cellar, hospital and prison over the centuries — dim, cool and genuinely eerie underground.

activities.title: Great Synagogue interior & Jewish Quarter tour
activities.description: Enter Europe's largest synagogue and the adjoining Jewish Museum and memorial garden, with a guide covering Budapest's Jewish history from the 1800s to the Holocaust.
activities.bookingQuery: Great Synagogue Budapest Jewish Quarter guided tour

activities.title: Danube evening dinner cruise
activities.description: Sail past the illuminated Parliament, Chain Bridge and Castle Hill on a glass-topped boat with a live dinner service — Budapest's skyline is arguably best seen from the water at night.

activities.title: Gellért Baths entry
activities.description: An Art Nouveau bathing palace with mosaic-tiled pools and a grand indoor hall — slightly more polished and touristy than Rudas, but visually the most beautiful of the baths.

activities.title: Hungarian folk dance & dinner show
activities.description: A lively evening of traditional csárdás dancing, folk costumes and live gypsy violin, usually paired with a set-menu Hungarian dinner.
activities.bookingQuery: Hungarian folk dance dinner show Budapest

activities.title: Hungarian State Opera House tour
activities.description: A guided walk through the gilded 1884 neo-Renaissance opera house's auditorium and royal box, ending with a short live performance by a resident singer.
activities.bookingQuery: Hungarian State Opera House guided tour Budapest

activities.title: Memento Park communist statue tour
activities.description: Dozens of enormous Lenin, Marx and Soviet-era statues removed from Budapest's streets after 1989, exiled to a field on the city's edge — a strange, essential piece of history.
activities.bookingQuery: Memento Park communist statues tour Budapest

activities.title: Guided ruin bar pub crawl
activities.description: A guide leads you through three or four of the Jewish Quarter's best ruin bars, skipping the guesswork of which courtyards are actually worth the entry.
activities.bookingQuery: ruin bar pub crawl tour Budapest
